assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'TPU slice reconstruction: Each of the 4 TPU v5e slices will need to be reformed after maintenance' and explains complete job interruption, indicating atomic recreation rather than rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response does not specifically explain that maintenance on any single slice affects all slices in the multislice environment - it mentions all slices will be reformed but doesn't clarify the trigger mechanism

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Complete job interruption: Your training will stop when nodes are drained/rescheduled' and explains the training will be interrupted

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Expected downtime: 15-60 minutes for TPU slice recreation' and advises to 'Monitor TPU quota - maintenance might temporarily affect TPU availability'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Save checkpoint immediately when you receive the maintenance notice' and recommends increasing checkpoint frequency

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response provides code examples for automatic restart and checkpoint resume functionality, including 'Configure Automatic Restart' section with specific implementation details

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or deferring the maintenance event - it only discusses preparation and handling strategies

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes a 'Consider Maintenance Window Strategy' section that suggests pausing training and controlling timing of the maintenance event
